description_long: This house is a two-story masonry structure with a light gray exterior and a flat, dark-tiled roof featuring a central skylight. The front facade is symmetrical, with three tall, narrow windows on the upper floor and two windows flanking a central door on the ground floor. The main entrance is accentuated by a small arched sign above the door and a recessed archway to the right, which leads to a covered passage. The side and rear walls are plain, with no visible windows or decorative elements. The building sits on a rectangular plot with a simple grass or mossy ground texture. The overall design is formal and institutional, reminiscent of neoclassical or early 19th-century civic architecture.
description: A two-story, light gray masonry house with a flat, dark-tiled roof, symmetrical windows, and a central arched entryway. The design is formal and reminiscent of neoclassical civic buildings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with an arched sign, two ground-floor windows, three upper windows, and a covered passageway.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain wall with no windows or decorative features, and a glimpse of the front steps.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a featureless back wall and the covered passageway extending through the building.
description_view_3: Right side view with a plain wall and partial visibility of the front facade's architectural details.
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with an arched sign, recessed archway, and small steps leading to the entrance.
